brief.query: Generate and validate a vtk.js streamline scene with browser evidence vtk.js Streamline Benchmark The candidate should represent streamline-like structure while preserving a deterministic render loop.
recommendedPatterns: Use slice, volume, streamline, and mag-iso as the benchmark backbone for vtk.js eval.
Seed streamline scenes explicitly and confirm vector-field inputs before validation.
Expect screenshot, console log, page error log, and a structured render verdict.
Treat browser evidence as the acceptance contract for vtk.js scene validation.
Build the scene in source -> mapper -> actor -> renderer order.
failureFixPairs: Capture artifact expectations per benchmark case so later eval output stays comparable.
If streamline output is empty, inspect seed generation and pipeline ordering before general browser repair.
If artifacts are missing, rerun the canonical Docker workflow instead of improvising a different browser command.
If validation fails early, retry with the minimal full-screen bootstrap scene first.
If the canvas is missing, reduce the page to a known-good bootstrap scene and compare new artifacts.
When the page says vtk is not defined, restore the runtime script include before changing scene logic.
brief.generationPrompt: Generate vtk.js code for the requested scene while preserving a deterministic browser-validation path. Scene kind: streamline. Use these retrieval-backed patterns: Use slice, volume, streamline, and mag-iso as the benchmark backbone for vtk.js eval. | Seed streamline scenes explicitly and confirm vector-field inputs before validation. | Expect screenshot, console log, page error log, and a structured render verdict. | Treat browser evidence as the acceptance contract for vtk.js scene validation. | Build the scene in source -> mapper -> actor -> renderer order. | Always call renderer.resetCamera() before renderWindow.render().. Avoid regressions captured by these failure-fix hints: Capture artifact expectations per benchmark case so later eval output stays comparable. | If streamline output is empty, inspect seed generation and pipeline ordering before general browser repair. | If artifacts are missing, rerun the canonical Docker workflow instead of improvising a different browser command. | If validation fails early, retry with the minimal full-screen bootstrap scene first. | If the canvas is missing, reduce the page to a known-good bootstrap scene and compare new artifacts. | When the page says vtk is not defined, restore the runtime script include before changing scene logic.. Acceptance contract: The scene should create a visible canvas during browser verification. | The script should end with renderer.resetCamera() followed by renderWindow.render(). | Browser evidence should include render-verification.json, browser-console.json, page-errors.json, and render-screenshot.png. | The browser console and page error logs should stay free of runtime errors. | The generated streamline workflow should make seed generation and vector-field wiring explicit..
acceptanceChecks: The scene should create a visible canvas during browser verification.
The script should end with renderer.resetCamera() followed by renderWindow.render().
Browser evidence should include render-verification.json, browser-console.json, page-errors.json, and render-screenshot.png.
The browser console and page error logs should stay free of runtime errors.
The generated streamline workflow should make seed generation and vector-field wiring explicit.

generatedCode.summary: vtk.js generated candidate for streamline scene

starterNotes: This generated candidate keeps a deterministic browser-validation path by default.
The browser script is ready for Phase 5 render verification without additional bundling steps.
The scene should create a visible canvas during browser verification.
The script should end with renderer.resetCamera() followed by renderWindow.render().
Browser evidence should include render-verification.json, browser-console.json, page-errors.json, and render-screenshot.png.
